information: Wet road surfaces can reduce tyre grip and increase braking distance. Drivers should reduce speed and leave more space between vehicles.
Driving in darkness can reduce visibility and make pedestrians, cyclists and road hazards harder to notice. Proper lighting and lower speeds can improve safety.
Higher vehicle speeds can increase stopping distance and collision impact. Following the speed limit and adjusting speed for road conditions can reduce risk.
Poor weather can affect visibility and vehicle control. Drivers should adjust speed, maintain safe distance and use suitable vehicle lights.
Junctions can create conflict points between vehicles and other road users. Drivers should check surroundings, follow signals and approach at a safe speed.
Pedestrians are vulnerable road users. Drivers should slow down near crossings, watch for people entering the road and give way where required.
Rural roads may have higher speed limits, bends and limited lighting. Drivers should adjust speed to visibility and road conditions.
Urban roads often have more pedestrians, cyclists, junctions and traffic. Drivers should remain alert and follow local speed limits.
